fuite_debut.title: Leave
fuite_debut.text:
Nobody's holding you.

It's almost worse.

Aryn's looking down.
Taera takes a step towards you, then stops.
Vael keeps his hands in the pockets.
Ikar don't move.

Your black blade hangs on your hand.

She's lighter than usual.

Or maybe it's you.

**"I have to leave. *

You don't know if you tell them that.

Or yourself.

Aryn slowly nods his head.

**"I know. *

And it hurts.

Because she seems to know a long time ago.

fuite_aryn.title: Aryn
fuite_aryn.text:
Aryn's approaching.

Not too much.

Just enough for you to hear his voice without others hearing everything.

**"Leave doesn't mean you have to disappear. *

You're almost laughing.

Not because it's funny.

Because it's a phrase from Aryn.

The kind of phrase that tries to open a door
Never force the handle.

**"If you want to come back..."**

It stops.

It's correcting.

**"When you want to come back. *

You're not answering.

She still understands.

fuite_ikar.title: Ikar
fuite_ikar.text:
Ikar doesn't say anything.

It's rare.

I mean, no.

It's Ikar.

But that silence is not the same.

He's looking at your black blade.

Then hers.

Then the ground.

**"I'm sorry. *

Again.

Same words.

Less violent than her blade under your throat.

Heavier.

You want to answer him.

Tell him you understand.
Or you don't understand.
Or you're mad at him.
Or you don't know.

Nothing comes out.

Ikar nods his head as if your silence was an answer.

Maybe it is.

taera_derniere_main.title: The Last Hand
taera_derniere_main.text:
Taera is finally moving forward.

She's giving you something.

A little scholarship.

Land.

**"Not from my village. "** she says.
**"From here. *
**"So you have a place to come back. *

You stare at his hand.

You could take it.

You could also refuse.

Vael coughs behind her.

**"I recall that free gifts are rare. *
**« Especially dusty gifts. *

Taera looks at him.

He's lifting his shoulders.

**"What? It's a nice dust. *

You smile in spite of yourself.

Just a moment.

vael_phrase.title: Vael's Phrase
vael_phrase.text:
Vael's coming with you a few steps.

Not far.

Just enough to pretend it's not a goodbye.

**"Good.** he said.
**"I'll say something very deep. *

He inspires.

Very seriously.

**« Avoid hostels with duck-shaped signs. *

You look at him.

**"Is that your deep sentence? *

**"No. *
**"That was a real advice. *

He smiles.

Then her smile barely moves.

**"The deep sentence is: when you're tired of being alone..."**
**"do not confuse shame with a closed door. *

The wind passes between you.

For once, Vael doesn't add anything.

route_seul.title: Road One
route_seul.narrator: The first days are almost easy.
route_seul.text:
You're walking north.

Not to Aurenthal.
Not to Erevane.
Not towards the Four.

Just the north.

The world is big when nobody tells you where to go.

The black blade remains silent.

The villages are far away.
The roads are empty.
Nights are cold, but bearable.

You sleep better.

Not good.

Better.

For three days, you think you found the answer.

No Order.
No prophecy.
No eyes that measure you.

Just you.

The wind.

The road.

And the blade that doesn't tremble anymore.

silence_paix.title: Almost Peace
silence_paix.text:
Silence has a form.

You didn't know that before.

It spreads in the hills.
He sits on the stones.
It fills the spaces that others' voices occupied.

Aryn doesn't ask questions.
Taera doesn't look at your hands.
Vael doesn't make a joke to hide what he understood.
Ikar is not watching your blade.

Nobody's waiting for you.

Nobody fears you.

Because there's no one here.

You close your eyes.

For the first time since Cendral,
you breathe without the impression
Someone count your breaths.

premiers_bandits.title: The Bandits
premiers_bandits.narrator: The first accident happened almost without noise.
premiers_bandits.text:
There are three of them.

Not soldiers.
Not monsters.
Not legendary murderers.

Three skinny men with rusty knives
And more fear than wickedness in the eyes.

**"The scholarship.** says the first.

You could run.

You could talk.

You could raise your hands.

But since Cendral,
Your body knows how to do something else before you.

The black blade comes out.

The violet light hits the trees.

A breath.

One.

When you open your eyes again,
The three men are on the ground.

Living.

But one of them screams.

His leg is stuck under a fallen trunk.

You didn't target the tree.

aider_bandit.title: Help
aider_bandit.text:
You're running towards him.

He sees you approaching with the black blade in his hand.

Her scream changes.

It's not pain anymore.

It's you.

**"No—no, don't approach—"**

You stop.

You put the blade on the floor.

You raise your hands.

**"I want to help. *

He can't hear you.

Or he can't.

You lift the trunk with the Chaos.

Too fast.

The violet force explodes in the earth.
The roots are tearing apart.
Stones are flying.

The man is free.

He crawls away from you crying.

You saved him.

He looks at you like you destroyed him.

partir_bandit.title: Leave
partir_bandit.text:
You're back.

You think they attacked you.

You think it's not your fault.

You think if they hadn't raised their knives,
Nothing would have happened.

The scream follows you between the trees.

Long time.

Longer than possible.

When he finally stops,
It's worse.

The silence has changed.

It's not empty anymore.

It contains something.

trace_violette.title: Trace
trace_violette.text:
You don't sleep well.

In the morning you go back in spite of yourself
to the accident site.

The earth is split.

The trees around them have black markings,
but not burned.

Absorbed.

As if the light had been removed from their bark.

You put your hand on a purple scar.

It's cold.

Your blade answers.

Not with words.

With a sensation.

Something like:

I can protect you. *

You take your hand off.

You don't know what scares you the most.

Let it be false.

Or be true.

eviter_routes.title: Far from the roads
eviter_routes.text:
You're avoiding roads.

Villages become lights in the distance.

You look at them from the hills.

Hot windows.
Fireplaces.
Voices.

People.

You stay outside.

It's better that way.

Nobody provokes you.
No one gets hurt.
Nobody sees the blade.

You don't eat much.
You sleep in pieces.
You're talking less and less.

After a week,
You realize you didn't hear your own voice.
Three days.

You're trying to say your name.

It sounds strange.

Like someone you knew.

village_grise.title: Griseval
village_grise.text:
The village is called Griseval.

You shouldn't have stopped.

But it's raining.
You're hungry.
And for a few hours,
You want to believe you can come in somewhere
Without the world stretching around you.

You hide the blade under your coat.

At the inn, nobody recognizes you.

Soup is too salty.
The bread is hard.
The bench squeaks.

It's the best meal of your life.

Then a little girl by the fireplace
Put your coat on.

**"Why does it make light underneath? *

Everyone turns to you.

verite_auberge.title: Truth
verite_auberge.text:
You open your coat.

The black blade absorbs the light from the room.

The flames of the chimney lean towards it.

Someone whispers:

**"Chaos. *

The word falls on the tables.

You raise your hands.

**"I don't want to hurt anyone. *

For a second,
Nobody moves.

Then the door opens violently.

A man comes running.

*"Wolfs are in the enclosures! *

The room explodes with noise.

And you,
before even thinking,
You're dating them.

sortie_auberge.title: Fleeing the Inn
sortie_auberge.text:
You're out too fast.

A chair falls behind you.
Someone screams.
The little girl's crying.

On the street, the rain hits your face.

Then you hear another cry.

Not in the inn.

Further.

To the pens.

Wolves.

Real.

You could leave now.

Nobody owes you anything.
You don't owe anyone anything.

Except you see a figure running towards the beasts.

A child.

Too small.

Too slow.

loups_enclos.title: Wolves
loups_enclos.text:
Wolves are not normal.

Their eyes shine with a white fever.
Their saliva smokes in the rain.

An old man says:

**"Malebrume. *

You don't know what that means.

You only know the child fell.

A wolf jumps.

You hit.

The Chaos answers.

Too loud.

The violet light crosses the enclosure,
the beast,
the barrier,
The barn behind.

The wolf disappears.

The barn is collapsing.

People are screaming underneath.

decombres.title: Under the Barn
decombres.text:
You run to the rubble.

Your blade is vibrating.

She wants to finish the fight.

You don't know.

You rip the beams one by one.
Too fast.
Not slow enough.

Taera would have known where to lay his hands.

Aryn would have said what to move first.

Vael would have found the child who doesn't cry.

Ikar would have kept the wolves away from you.

But they're not here.

You're alone.

You get two people out alive.

The third one doesn't move.

Rain washes the dust on his face.

Someone behind you whispers:

**"You saved us. *

Then, below:

**"But look around you. *

tuer_loups.title: Ending the Fight
tuer_loups.text:
You turn to the other wolves.

You don't think anymore.

It's easier that way.

The black blade traces purple arcs in the rain.
Every strike rips off the earth.
Every dodge breaks something.
Every breath of the Chaos expands the circle of disaster.

When the last wolf falls,
There are no more pens.

No barn.

No more silence either.

Crying.
Cries.
Calls under the beams.

You won.

That's the problem.

partir_griseval.title: Do not turn around
partir_griseval.text:
You're leaving.

The screams stay behind.

You think it wasn't your fight.

Every time you approach,
Someone's in pain.

So leave,
Maybe it's helping.

The rain makes the road dark.

Your blade is cold against your leg.

After an hour,
You're stopping.

You forgot the taste of soup.

You didn't forget the child's cry.

matin_griseval.title: The Morning After
matin_griseval.text:
In the morning, Griseval counts the damage.

Three wounded.
A dead woman.
Two houses cracked.
The barn destroyed.
The dead wolves.

The child lives.

His mother thanks you.

She shakes when she does.

The village chief doesn't know where to put his eyes.

**"We know what you did. *
**"We also know what happened. *

He inspires.

**"Go before others wake up. *

No anger.

No hatred.

Just tired fear.

It's worse.

honte.title: The Shame
honte.text:
You walk until you don't feel your legs anymore.

Griseval disappears behind the hills.

You saved a child.
You killed the wolves.
You stopped worse.

And yet,
a woman died under a barn
that your power dropped.

You're thinking about the Four.

To their eyes.
To their hands.
To their voices around the fire.

They weren't just here to watch you.

They were there to limit the damage.

To be what you're not yet.

A thought goes through your mind.

I could come back. *

She won't stay long.

Shame hunting.

You keep going.

orage.title: The storm
orage.text:
The storm is coming without warning.

The wind sets the grass.
The rain strikes from the side.
Lightning falls away, then closer.

You find refuge under a stone arch.

The black blade lights itself.

Violet in the night.

You stare at her.

**"Stop. *

She's not stopping.

The thunder is responding.

Or maybe it's you.

The purple light goes up along your arm.

Not a stroke.

Not yet.

An overflow.

Like a full glass we keep filling.

choices.text: Plant the blade in the ground.

ancrer_seul.title: S'Ancrer Alone
ancrer_seul.text:
You plant the blade in the ground.

As Taera taught you.

Both hands on the handle.
Knees in the mud.
Low breathing.

**"I'm here. *

The sentence tastes like his voice.

The earth is shaking.

Not enough.

You're not Taera.
You're not Earth.

You still hold the Chaos.

Part of it goes down to the ground.
Another one stays in your bones.

Morning,
The tray around you is split
Like an old plate.

But nothing burned.

No one was there.

It's a victory.

A victory without a witness.

laisser_sortir.title: Let Out
laisser_sortir.text:
You stop holding back.

For a second,
It's almost a relief.

The Chaos is in a circle.

The rain disappears before touching the ground.
The stones burst.
The ark above you breaks and goes up into the sky
As if the world fell backwards.

You scream.

No anger.

Fatigue.

When everything stops,
The storm is gone.

The set, too.

There's still a crater.

And you in the center.

Get up.

Intact.

That's what terrifies you the most.

rumeurs.title: The Rumors
rumeurs.text:
Rumors run faster than you.

A man with a black blade.
A purple light in the mountains.
A village saved and broken.
A crater on a plateau where there was only one arch.

You hear your story in others' mouths.

She changes every time.

In one version, you're a monster.
In another, a savior.
In a third, a sign of the end.

None of them said you were scared.

None of them say you tried.

None of them say you wanted to
Someone be there to tell you what to do next.

choices.text: Look for a place where no one lives.

caravane.title: The Caravan
caravane.text:
The caravan is attacked by looters.

This time,
You wait.

You're watching.

You choose.

Aryn would have approved.

You hit the ground in front of the horses.
Not men.
Not the wheels.

The Chaos opens a trench between the looters and the caravan.

Perfect.

Almost.

A freaky horse.
The first cart turns.
Cases are falling.
A wheel breaks.

Nobody dies.

But a merchant is looking at you,
White face.

**"Thank you. *

He says it like we give a play to a wolf
so he could go further.

choices.text: Accept that it is as best as possible.
Promise not to help anyone anymore.

frontiere_neige.title: The White Frontier
frontiere_neige.text:
You hit the mountains when the snow starts.

Up there,
Villages are rare.
Roads disappear.
Names too.

Nobody's following you.

Not the Four.
Not Erevane.
Not rumors.

Or maybe they're still following,
But too far to touch you.

You find an abandoned cabin
by a cliff.

The roof doesn't hold.
The door squeaks.
The fireplace smokes inside.

That's perfect.

Not because it's beautiful.

Because it's far away.

plus_loin.title: Even More Far
plus_loin.text:
You keep going.

Still further.

There's something reassuring.
in the idea that there is always a place
Even less inhabited than the previous one.

More tracks.
No more smoke.
No more voices.

One night, you find a cave open on the void.

The wind sings like Vael
When he pretended not to be sad.

You're laughing.

A real laugh.

Then you stop.

Because nobody heard it.

cabane.title: Far from the World
cabane.text:
Months pass.

You learn to live with little.

Fire without magic.
Repair a roof.
Trap a hare.
Recognise the clouds that announce a storm.

The Chaos calm down.

Or he falls asleep.

You don't know anymore.

Sometimes you dream of the Four.

Aryn's asking you if you're sleeping.
Taera puts a blanket on your shoulders.
Ikar stays by the door.
Vael tells an absurd story with a duck, a king and a boot.

You wake up with a tight throat.

The cabin is empty.

The black blade too.

choices.text: Write their names in the wood.

noms_bois.title: Names
noms_bois.text:
You take Marek's sculpting knife.

Your hands are shaking.

On the table, in the worn wood of the cabin,
You're serious about four names.

Aryn.

A takes time.
You want it to be good.

Ikar.

More direct.
Like him.

Taera.

You have to stop between the two syllables.

Vael.

The V's out of the way.
You do it again.
Twice.

Then you stay here.
The knife in the hand.
The four names in the wood.

You touch every letter.

The water doesn't scream.
Fire's coming.
The earth is on.
The air's passing.

Lessons you didn't ask for.

That you still wear.

You put the knife away.

You don't know why you did this.

Yes.

You know.

effacer.title: Don't think
effacer.text:
You put away everything that reminds others.

Land exchange.
A piece of blue fabric.
A feather you don't remember keeping.
A stone blackened by fire.

You put them in a box.

Then under the floor.

Then you put a table on it.

This is ridiculous.

As if memories needed a door
to get out.

That night,
You're not dreaming.

Morning,
You don't know if it's better.

You don't know what better means.

annees.title: Years
annees.text:
We're talking.

Let a silhouette sometimes walk on the ridges.
Let the storms change course.
Let monsters avoid some valleys.
That lost travelers find a fire already on,
Then nobody around.

It is also said that you should not look for the silhouette.

Not because she's attacking.

Because the world around it breaks.

A bridge that gives way.
An avalanche that leaves too soon.
A river freezing in the summer.

Nothing voluntary.

Never volunteered.

But people learn.

They survive better
when they keep their distance.

choices.text: See what happens to the Four.

quatre_recherche.title: Those Who Seek
quatre_recherche.text:
The Four are looking for a long time.

Aryn reads all the rumors.
Taera follows the broken land.
Ikar climbs every road where someone saw a purple light.
Vael talks to people who say they haven't seen anything.

Especially them.

For years,
They always arrive too late.

A fire still warm.
A footprint in the snow.
A table engraved with five names in an empty cabin.

The day they find the table,
Nobody's talking.

Even Vael.

Especially Vael.

Taera put her hand on your name.

Aryn closes his eyes.

Ikar's going outside.

The wind passes through the cabin.

And for once,
He's not joking.

horizon.title: The Horizon
horizon.text:
The world survives.

It's already a lot.

You look at him from a cliff
where the cards no longer have names.

The sea is below.

Grey.
Large.
Indifferent.

Your black blade is on your belt.

She hasn't vibrated in months.

Or she vibrates so gently
That you learned not to make a difference.

You bring out Marek's knife.

You open it.

You shut up.

There was Cendral.
There was the crossroads.
There was a leak.

There was Griseval.
The storm.
The cabin.
The names in the wood.

And now this cliff.

The wind.

The sea.

Somewhere Aerith continues.

Cities are rebuilding.
People remember.
Maybe the four are still looking.

Or maybe they figured it out.

That some paths need time.

And that some storms
end up calming down
alone.

Slowly.

But alone.

paix_courte.title: A Evening Without Nothing
paix_courte.text:
That night you found a clearing.

Little.
Protected from the wind.

You're making a fire.

Kid.
No magic.
With your hands.

Like Ikar showed you.
*The fire follows the intent. *

You think of him by hitting the flint.

Fire's on.

You eat.

You look at the stars.

The black blade is laid away.

Tonight she doesn't vibrate.

Tonight nothing happens.

Just a fire.
Stars.
And the silence of a sleeping forest.

You're thinking of Cendral.

At the nightlights in the square.

That doesn't look like it.

But it looks a bit.

penser_eux_nuit.title: In the Fire
penser_eux_nuit.text:
You look at the flames.

Ikar would say the fire thinks for you.

Aryn would say the water around you is listening to you.

Taera would say the earth under you is carrying you.

Vael would say something funny
So you can stop hurting yourself.

They're not here.

But their voices are there.

The sentences they said.
The gestures they did.

You wonder if they're looking.

Probably.

Aryn's still looking.

You stretch your legs.

The fire crackles.

You've been sleeping better than long ago.

noms_longtemps.title: Stay Here
noms_longtemps.text:
You sit in front of the table.

Long time.

The names.

You're thinking about what Aryn would have told you.
The doubt is water. He ends up finding his way. *

That way brought you here.

In an unnamed cabin.
In mountains without maps.

With four names engraved in wood.

That's not what she imagined.

That's not what you imagined.

But the names are here.

And that means something.

Something you can't formulate yet.

Maybe someday.
